name: Olive Tree Cafe
tagline: The only legit Greek food on Oahu — cash only, BYOB, counter service, and a 20-year local cult following.
mustOrder: souvlaki plate — chicken or lamb, both excellent
fresh fish special — ask what's available
parking: Small lot on Kilauea Ave; street parking on side streets
talkStory: Opened in the late 1990s by Savas Mojarrad, an Iranian-born chef who brought Mediterranean flavors to Kahala. Over 25 years later, it remains the only dedicated Greek restaurant on Oahu. Cash only, BYOB with no corkage, counter service — the format hasn't changed because regulars won't let it. The fresh fish specials use whatever is best at the Honolulu Fish Auction that morning. Zagat-rated and consistently voted Honolulu's best Mediterranean. Located next to Kapa Hale, making a two-stop dinner easy.
